# Admin Shell Module LLM Guide

Purpose: schema-driven admin CRUD over the host app's existing D1 tables. You
declare resources; the module provides governed list/get/create/update/delete.
The UI is rendered by the host app.

Entry points:
- `src/index.ts` exports the safe public surface.
- `src/registry.ts` defines resources (table, columns, permissions, softDelete).
- `src/authz.ts` is the permission gate (hasPermission, wildcard "*").
- `src/validate.ts` coerces/validates values against a definition (editable only).
- `src/use-cases/` holds framework-neutral behavior: listRecords, getRecord,
  createRecord, updateRecord, deleteRecord.
- `src/ports/` defines TableGateway.
- `src/adapters/` contains a D1 gateway (identifier-whitelisted SQL) and a memory gateway.
- `module.json` is the machine-readable manifest. The module owns no tables.

Model:
- Platform module. ResourceDefinitions are the only source of SQL identifiers, so
  the generic query builder is injection-safe.

Correctness invariants (the point of the module — do not remove):
- Every action checks the resource read/write permission (hasPermission).
- delete honors softDelete config (no always-hard-delete).
- Writes accept only editable columns (validateValues); unknown fields rejected.
- list is one query + one count with clamped pagination (no N+1).

Agent rules:
- Keep use cases neutral; data access behind TableGateway. UI lives in the host.
- Never interpolate request input into SQL; identifiers come from the registry.
- Never make real I/O in tests; inject createMemoryTableGateway().
- Ask before changing data mutations, permission logic, or production deploy.
